name: Union Find (Disjoint Set Union)
description:
A Disjoint Set Union (DSU), also known as Union-Find, is a data structure that keeps track of elements partitioned into a number of disjoint (non-overlapping) sets.

It provides two primary operations:

  • find(i): Determine which set element i belongs to. This can be used for determining if two elements are in the same set.
  • union(i, j): Join two disjoint sets into a single set.

The visualization demonstrates Path Compression and Union by Rank/Size, which optimize the structure to nearly constant time per operation.
